body_txt: This is a Butt-Monkey whose misfortune is well-deserved and a repeated case of Laser-Guided Karma . Maybe they're just enough of a Jerkass . But far more frequently, they are a full-blown baddie. If the character is an outright villain, they are generally a particularly comedic Card-Carrying Villain or, if not that , the Laughable Lackey to his master's Vile Villain. They are rarely, if ever , a Complete Monster ; Magnificent Bastards likewise tend to be Immune to Slapstick . As such, this can make the Karmic Butt-Monkey come off as more pitiable than anything else , becoming easily mockable for the fandom. The Karmic Butt-Monkey's natural enemy is often the Unintentionally Karmic , a Nice Guy character who offers Unwanted Assistance and friendship to the Butt-Monkey, only for their actions to set off Disaster Dominoes or some other horrible effect that always ends in suffering for the bad guys. A Sub-Trope of Butt-Monkey and Laser-Guided Karma . Sister trope to Asshole Victim (though that's also a Super-Trope ) but they don't die, and while this trope is a guest character or someone only seen getting karma once, the Karmic Butt-Monkey is a main/recurring character who has a Running Gag of "does something mean or bad and getting the short end of the stick because of it". May become Unintentionally Sympathetic if the Butt-Monkey part overlaps too much the Jerkass part. Compare Unsympathetic Comedy Protagonist , when the only difference is that this character isn't necessarily the protagonist. It is more than likely that the subject comes across as an Ineffectual Sympathetic Villain . Compare Jerkass Woobie , sometimes they overlap, though. Often the victim of Contempt Crossfire and Rudeness Backfire as well. If the character eventually dies (and becoming an Asshole Victim ), expect to be a Karmic Death , of course. Compare and contrast Ineffectual Sympathetic Villain , a villainous character who fails so often and so hard (often resulting in them harming themselves) that you can't help but pity them, and yet there is all too often overlapping, and they end up both laughed at and pitied by the audience. Contrast Kafka Komedy , where pain all-too-often happens to someone who especially doesn't deserve it, Karmic Overkill , where the audience thinks a character doesn't deserve the retribution that they get, and Designated Monkey where the audience thinks the slapstick of the resident Butt-Monkey is overbearing (karmic or not) and doesn't deserve the ridicule and injuries they get.
